root_text.sn4.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn4.6:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ abbhokāse nisinno hoti, devo ca ekamekaṁ phusāyati.
root_text.sn4.6:2.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā bhagavato bhayaṁ chambhitattaṁ lomahaṁsaṁ uppādetukāmo mahantaṁ sapparājavaṇṇaṁ abhinimminitvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn4.6:2.8: Seyyathāpi nāma deve gaḷagaḷāyante vijjullatā niccharanti;
root_text.sn4.6:2.10: Seyyathāpi nāma kammāragaggariyā dhamamānāya saddo hoti;
root_text.sn4.6:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.6:7.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ bhagavā, jānāti maṁ sugato”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn4.6:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was meditating in the open during the dark of night, while a gentle rain drizzled down.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.1: Then Māra the Wicked, wanting to make the Buddha feel fear, terror, and goosebumps, manifested in the form of a huge serpent king and approached him.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.2: Its body was like a huge canoe carved from a single tree.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.4: Its hood was like a large brewer’s sieve.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.6: Its eyes were like big bronze cups from Kosala.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.8: Its tongue flickered from its mouth like lightning flashes in a thunderstorm.
translation_text.sn4.6:2.10: The sound of its breathing was like the puffing of a blacksmith’s bellows.
translation_text.sn4.6:3.1: Then the Buddha, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, replied to him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.6:5.1: Though there are lots of creepy crawlies,
translation_text.sn4.6:5.2: and lots of flies and snakes,
translation_text.sn4.6:5.4: of a great sage in that empty hut.
translation_text.sn4.6:6.1: Though the firmament may split <j>and the earth may quake,
translation_text.sn4.6:6.2: and all creatures be stricken with fear;
translation_text.sn4.6:6.3: and even if an arrow’s aimed at their breast,
translation_text.sn4.6:6.4: the Buddhas take no shelter in attachments.”
translation_text.sn4.6:7.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The Buddha knows me! The Holy One knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.

variant_text.sn4.6:5.2: ḍaṁsasarīsapā → ḍaṁsasiriṁsapā (bj, sya-all, km); ḍaṁsā siriṁsapā (pts1ed, pts2ed)
